feedback.text: Please also include customer protections for digital games that get shut down by publishers in a way that they become unplayable. Many games have been destroyed by this practice (shutting down games without an end-of-life plan). And many more are at risk of being shut down. Because there are currently no laws stopping publishers from doing so. People pay for the game, and then the publishers shut them down, and you can't play them anymore, when the games only work with an active server provided by the publishers. Consumers should at least be given the tools to keep the games running by themselves.
